title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, achieving comparable quality to much larger competitors. The model is available as E2B and E4B variants for on-device deployment on Pixel phones and enables efficient deployment in medical and multi-agent scenarios.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4, framing open models as a path to sovereign ownership of AI capabilities. The talk covers the 31B model, which ranks fourth on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU and reaching quality comparable to much larger competitors. It also details the E2B and E4B variants built for on-device deployment, including running on Pixel phones. The case for open models extends to efficient deployment in medical and multi-agent scenarios, with model compression and on-device AI as the enabling techniques.
